title: Vercel API token committed to .env.local in plaintext
impact: Anyone with repository access has a live Vercel credential that can deploy, tear down projects, or read other production environment variables including database credentials in a healthcare context
evidence: HELIX_VERCEL_TOKEN=vcp_1Nb4zesMHVDtft3qtcWtGXUeTs7sE2nwzqYKiYcmbYg2FQ37a52szwxD committed in plaintext
fix_suggestion: Revoke this token immediately in the Vercel dashboard. Store it only in GitHub Actions secrets. Add .env.local to .gitignore and add a secret scanning step to CI.

title: No test script in package.json — all 103 play functions never execute in CI
impact: The entire Storybook interaction test layer is dead. turbo test skips this package entirely, meaning CI runs zero Storybook interaction tests. Play functions verifying form submit, select keyboard nav, and card click can be broken for weeks without detection.
evidence: scripts block has only dev, build, build-storybook, clean — no test entry. vitest.config.ts exists and is properly configured but is never invoked.
fix_suggestion: Add 'test': 'vitest run' to package.json scripts. Also add vitest and @vitest/browser as direct devDependencies.

title: vitest not declared as direct dependency — phantom resolution from hx-library
impact: vitest resolves today only because packages/hx-library hoists it. Any deduplication, lockfile change, or workspace restructure silently breaks the import of vitest/config and Vitest globals in vitest.setup.ts.
evidence: devDependencies has no vitest entry. vitest.config.ts imports from vitest/config; vitest.setup.ts uses beforeAll global — both depend on phantom resolution.
fix_suggestion: Add 'vitest': '^3.0.0' and '@vitest/browser': '^3.0.0' to devDependencies in /workspace/apps/storybook/package.json.

title: all: unset CSS removes focus ring from all interactive buttons with no replacement
impact: Keyboard users activating copy-to-clipboard buttons in token documentation pages receive no visible focus indicator — a direct WCAG 2.1 AA SC 2.4.7 violation. The same pattern exists in Typography.mdx.
evidence: .hx-token-colors button { all: unset; box-sizing: border-box; } — no :focus-visible rule defined anywhere after the reset. Identical pattern in Typography.mdx lines 32-55.
fix_suggestion: Add .hx-token-colors button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--hx-color-primary-500, #2563EB); outline-offset: 2px; } after every all: unset block that targets buttons.

title: Copy-to-clipboard buttons in token pages have no accessible name
impact: Screen reader users encounter dozens of buttons whose visible text is a truncated token key with no announcement of their copy-to-clipboard purpose. title attribute is not a reliable accessible name — VoiceOver on macOS does not consistently read it. Violates WCAG 4.1.2.
evidence: <button title={`Click to copy: ${token.name}`} onClick={() => copyToClipboard(token.name)}>{token.name.replace('--hx-color-', '')}</button> — no aria-label, no visually-hidden span. Same pattern in Typography.mdx.
fix_suggestion: Replace title with aria-label={`Copy ${token.name} to clipboard`}. Add a role='status' live region to announce 'Copied!' after clipboard write succeeds.

title: No Content Security Policy or security headers in vercel.json
impact: No CSP restricts script execution in story iframes. No X-Frame-Options prevents clickjacking. No X-Content-Type-Options prevents MIME sniffing. For a healthcare design system site that renders component previews in iframes and loads external fonts, these are non-negotiable.
evidence: headers array contains only X-Robots-Tag. Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.
fix_suggestion: Add CSP, X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, and Permissions-Policy headers to the vercel.json headers array.

title: Google Fonts loaded without Subresource Integrity in preview-head.html and manager-head.html
impact: If Google CDN is compromised or DNS hijacked, malicious CSS or font resources could be delivered to every Storybook session. CSS injection can execute JavaScript in some browser versions. Supply chain risk for a healthcare infrastructure tool.
evidence: <link href='https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700;900&display=swap' rel='stylesheet' /> — no integrity attribute. Same in manager-head.html.
fix_suggestion: Self-host the Inter font using @fontsource/inter or download .woff2 files into public/fonts/. Remove the Google Fonts link tags entirely to eliminate the external CDN dependency, privacy risk, and air-gapped network incompatibility.

title: Storybook deployed publicly without access control in healthcare context
impact: MDX documentation describes patient admission forms, MRN fields, medication order workflows, EHR URLs, PHI field structures, and internal system naming conventions. The site is publicly accessible with only advisory robots.txt protection — malicious actors can access detailed healthcare system implementation patterns.
evidence: vercel.json has no authentication or password field. manager-head.html sets noindex meta but no access gate enforces it. robots.txt Disallow:/ is advisory only.
fix_suggestion: Enable Vercel Password Protection or Vercel SSO for this deployment. For healthcare organizations, IP allowlisting to internal network ranges is the more robust control.

title: debug-storybook.log with internal filesystem paths committed to repository
impact: The log exposes the developer's full local filesystem path /Volumes/Development/wc-2026/, OS (macOS), volume label, project structure, and internal package names pre-rename. Debug logs in source control escalate: future sessions may capture env vars or credentials.
evidence: [01:07:10.081] [DEBUG] Getting package.json info for /Volumes/Development/wc-2026/apps/storybook/package.json...
fix_suggestion: Delete debug-storybook.log and run git rm --cached apps/storybook/debug-storybook.log. Add *.log and debug-*.log to root .gitignore. Add a pre-commit hook rejecting staged log files.

title: GitHub Actions workflows use floating @v4 action tags instead of pinned SHA digests
impact: v4 tags can be moved by action maintainers at any time. If upstream repositories are compromised (a known supply chain attack vector), malicious code can be injected into the CI environment which has access to all repository secrets and deployment credentials.
evidence: uses: actions/checkout@v4, uses: actions/setup-node@v4, uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 — mutable version tags in ci.yml and ci-matrix.yml.
fix_suggestion: Pin all actions to their full commit SHA: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 (v4.2.2). Use Renovate or Dependabot to keep pinned SHAs updated automatically.

title: Hero banner titles use non-semantic div elements breaking screen reader heading navigation
impact: Screen reader users cannot navigate to page titles using heading navigation (H key in NVDA/JAWS, Rotor in VoiceOver). No document hierarchy cue is provided for the primary topic of each page. Pattern repeats across hx-form.mdx, hx-select.mdx, and BestPractices.mdx.
evidence:
hx-card Component
...
— plain divs with no ARIA role or heading semantics.
fix_suggestion:
Change .hero-title divs to

elements and .hero-desc divs to

elements across all four MDX files.

title: a11y addon not configured to auto-run — axe scans only on manual panel open
impact: For a healthcare library with a zero-regression mandate, axe violations are only surfaced when a developer manually opens the Accessibility tab. Violations in production components will not be caught during normal story browsing or CI runs.
evidence: a11y parameter block only enables color-contrast rule. No initialGlobals.a11y: { manual: false } configured. No automatic axe execution on story render.
fix_suggestion: Add initialGlobals: { a11y: { manual: false } } to the Preview parameters in preview.ts. Expand a11y.config.rules to cover the full WCAG 2.1 AA ruleset (label, button-name, image-alt, aria-required-attr, landmark-one-main).

title: No skip navigation link in Storybook preview or manager
impact: WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) requires a mechanism to skip repeated navigation. Storybook's sidebar and toolbar must be tabbed through before reaching story content. No skip link exists in either preview-head.html or manager-head.html.
evidence: preview-head.html contains only font preconnect links — no skip link. manager-head.html contains only meta robots and font loading.
fix_suggestion: Add a visually-hidden skip link to preview-head.html: Skip to story content with CSS positioning that makes it visible on focus.

title: No lang attribute set on preview document
impact: WCAG 3.1.1 (Language of Page) requires lang on the html element. Screen readers rely on lang to select the correct speech synthesis engine. In a healthcare context where clinical terminology must be pronounced correctly, this is significant.
evidence: preview-head.html contains only font links — no lang injection. manager-head.html has no lang attribute either.
fix_suggestion: Add <script>document.documentElement.setAttribute('lang', 'en');</script> to preview-head.html to set lang on the preview iframe document before story rendering.

title: Hero description text fails WCAG 4.5:1 contrast — rgba(255,255,255,0.45) on dark background
impact: WCAG 1.4.3 requires 4.5:1 for normal text. rgba(255,255,255,0.45) on #0a0e27 computes to approximately rgb(116,116,131) on near-black, yielding ~3.2:1 — below the minimum. Body text at 0.95rem requires the full 4.5:1 threshold.
evidence: .hx-intro-hero .hx-intro-hero-desc { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.45) !important; } — on hero gradient background starting at #0a0e27.
fix_suggestion: Raise opacity to rgba(255,255,255,0.80) or use a fixed value such as #c8cde8 which yields approximately 5.1:1 on #0a0e27.

title: Feature chip text fails WCAG 4.5:1 contrast — rgba(255,255,255,0.5) on dark hero background
impact: Chip labels (Lit 3.x, WCAG 2.1 AA, TypeScript Strict) at 0.6875rem render at ~3.0:1 contrast. Text at 11px requires the full 4.5:1 minimum, not the 3:1 large-text threshold.
evidence: .hx-intro-hero .hx-intro-chip { font-size: 0.6875rem !important; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.5) !important; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.04) !important; }
fix_suggestion: Increase chip text opacity to at least 0.85 or use #d1d5db which achieves approximately 7.5:1 on the dark hero background.

title: Manager toolbar text color #6c757d fails 4.5:1 contrast on white background
impact: barTextColor: '#6c757d' on barBg: '#ffffff' yields approximately 4.48:1 — just below the 4.5:1 WCAG AA minimum. Storybook toolbar text labels (story names, addon tab labels) fail contrast for developers using assistive technology.
evidence: const helixTheme = create({ barTextColor: '#6c757d', barBg: '#ffffff' }) — contrast ratio ~4.48:1.
fix_suggestion: Darken to '#555555' (7:1 on white) or minimum '#616161' (4.54:1 on white).

title: Missing tsconfig.json — Storybook TypeScript files excluded from strict type checking
impact: main.ts, manager.ts, preview.ts, vitest.setup.ts, and vitest.config.ts are transpiled without strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, or exactOptionalPropertyTypes. npm run type-check at the monorepo level cannot verify this workspace. TypeScript errors accumulate silently.
evidence: ls /workspace/apps/storybook/tsconfig.json — file does not exist. Root tsconfig.base.json defines strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true.
fix_suggestion: Create /workspace/apps/storybook/tsconfig.json extending ../../tsconfig.base.json, including .storybook/**/*.ts, vitest.config.ts, stories/**/*.ts in the include array.

title: console.log in live script tag inside hx-form.mdx executes in preview iframe
impact: The inline <script> block at line 540-563 contains console.log('Form data:', e.detail.values) and console.error('Form submission error:', error) on executable paths. This script executes in Storybook's preview iframe. Not documentation — live JavaScript.
evidence: <!-- hx-form.mdx inside live <script> tag --> console.log('Form data:', e.detail.values); console.error('Form submission error:', error);
fix_suggestion: Remove the inline <script> block from the MDX entirely. JavaScript integration examples should live inside fenced code blocks (which are inert) rather than live script tags.

title: hx-submit event detail documents Record<string, any> violating no-any policy
impact: Public API documentation shows { formData: FormData, values: Record<string, any> }. Consumers who follow this type for event handlers will write untyped code. The zero-any mandate applies to documentation examples as well as source.
evidence: | `hx-submit` | `{ formData: FormData, values: Record<string, any> }` | Fired when the form is submitted |
fix_suggestion: Update documentation to Record<string, unknown>. Verify the component implementation in packages/hx-library uses unknown not any in its event detail interface.

title: Wrong package for setProjectAnnotations in vitest.setup.ts
impact: @storybook/addon-vitest documentation requires setProjectAnnotations from @storybook/addon-vitest, not from the framework package @storybook/web-components. Using the framework package may not correctly initialize the Vitest-specific lifecycle hooks, causing stories to render outside the Vitest browser environment.
evidence: import { setProjectAnnotations } from '@storybook/web-components'; — should be from '@storybook/addon-vitest'.
fix_suggestion: Change to: import { setProjectAnnotations } from '@storybook/addon-vitest';

title: sleep 5 race condition in CI VRT step — non-deterministic server readiness
impact: On slow GitHub runners or under load, 5 seconds is insufficient for http-server to bind its port. This is a textbook flaky test pattern: passes on fast machines, fails intermittently on loaded CI runners. The zero-tolerance flaky test policy makes this a blocker.
evidence: npx http-server apps/storybook/dist -p 3151 & sleep 5 npm run test:vrt — bare sleep with no readiness probe.
fix_suggestion: Replace sleep 5 with: npx wait-on http://localhost:3151 --timeout 30000. Add wait-on as a pinned root devDependency.

title: MDX story files have zero play functions — docs layer entirely untested
impact: hx-card.mdx, hx-form.mdx, hx-select.mdx, BestPractices.mdx, and all token MDX pages contain no Canvas or Story blocks with play functions. The documentation layer has zero automated interaction coverage. Behaviors described in prose (form submit, select navigation, card click) are never exercised.
evidence: All component MDX files import only Meta from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'. No Canvas, Story, or play function references exist in any MDX file.
fix_suggestion: Either embed Canvas blocks referencing named story exports with play functions, or explicitly document that MDX pages are documentation-only and interaction tests are owned by .stories.ts files.

title: CI Storybook build output never verified before VRT runs
impact: If the Storybook build silently fails or produces incomplete output, the VRT step immediately serves and tests a broken artifact. No step checks that apps/storybook/dist/index.html was actually produced.
evidence: 'Build Storybook' step runs npm run build:storybook with no subsequent verification. 'Serve Storybook and run VRT' immediately starts http-server against the (potentially empty) dist directory.
fix_suggestion: Add a verification step: test -f apps/storybook/dist/index.html && test -f apps/storybook/dist/iframe.html || exit 1 between the build and VRT steps.

title: Turborepo remote caching not configured — CI runs full rebuilds on every push
impact: Every CI run rebuilds the entire monorepo from scratch. The Storybook Vite build is the most expensive step. Without remote cache, full rebuild time is paid on every PR — particularly costly given the 15-minute CI timeout.
evidence: ci.yml has no TURBO_TOKEN or TURBO_TEAM env vars. turbo.json has no remoteCache configuration block.
fix_suggestion: Add TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }} and TURBO_TEAM: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TEAM }} to CI env block. Configure the Vercel Remote Cache token and team slug in GitHub Actions repository secrets.

title: @storybook/blocks@8.6.15 incompatible with Storybook 10.2.8
impact: Storybook doctor reports: @storybook/blocks@8.6.15 which depends on ^8.6.15 is incompatible with Storybook 10.2.8. This can cause story rendering failures, broken autodocs, and unpredictable behavior in the docs addon.
evidence: [WARN] 1 issue found: You are currently using Storybook 10.2.8 but have packages which are incompatible: @storybook/blocks@8.6.15
fix_suggestion: Run npm ls @storybook/blocks to identify the source. Add to root package.json: overrides: { '@storybook/blocks': '^10.2.8' }. Run npm install and verify with npx storybook doctor.

title: Orphaned storybook:build Turborepo task defined but never invoked
impact: turbo.json defines storybook:build but all CI and root scripts invoke turbo build --filter=@helix/storybook (the generic build task). The intentional storybook:build config is never utilized; its cache outputs are never read. Cache tracking falls under the generic build task accidentally.
evidence: turbo.json: 'storybook:build': { dependsOn: ['^build'], outputs: ['dist/**'] }. package.json: 'build:storybook': 'turbo build --filter=@helix/storybook' — calls build task, not storybook:build.
fix_suggestion: Either rename package.json script to storybook:build so turbo run storybook:build works, or remove the orphaned storybook:build entry from turbo.json and rely on the generic build task.

title: Google Fonts loaded as render-blocking synchronous stylesheet in both frames
impact: The font <link rel='stylesheet'> is synchronous and render-blocking in both the preview iframe and manager UI. On slow or offline connections this blocks story rendering entirely. 6 weight variants loaded when only 4 are needed.
evidence: <link href='https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700;900&display=swap' rel='stylesheet' /> — no rel='preload' with onload pattern. Identical in manager-head.html.
fix_suggestion: Self-host Inter via @fontsource/inter (resolves SRI, privacy, and offline concerns simultaneously). If external loading is retained, use the preload/onload non-blocking pattern and narrow weights to 400;500;600;700.

title: No Vite chunk splitting or optimization configured in viteFinal
impact: Without manualChunks configuration, Vite may bundle all MDX story content into a single large chunk. No optimizeDeps.include forces pre-bundling of @helix/tokens. No build.sourcemap: false for production. Degrades initial load performance.
evidence: viteFinal returns config after only adding two define entries — no build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks, no optimizeDeps, no build.sourcemap setting.
fix_suggestion: Extend viteFinal to add manualChunks splitting @helix/tokens into its own chunk. Set build.sourcemap: false. Add optimizeDeps.include for @helix/tokens to force pre-bundling.

title: 11 component MDX overview documentation pages missing
impact: 14 components exist in the library (hx-alert, hx-badge, hx-button, hx-card, hx-checkbox, hx-container, hx-form, hx-prose, hx-radio-group, hx-radio, hx-select, hx-switch, hx-text-input, hx-textarea) but only 3 have MDX overview pages. 11 components have no usage guidelines, accessibility notes, CSS parts reference, or Drupal integration documentation.
evidence: ls /workspace/apps/storybook/stories/components/ returns only hx-card.mdx, hx-form.mdx, hx-select.mdx. 11 component directories confirmed in packages/hx-library/src/components/ have no corresponding MDX docs.
fix_suggestion: Create MDX overview pages for: hx-button, hx-text-input, hx-alert, hx-badge, hx-checkbox, hx-radio-group, hx-switch, hx-textarea, hx-container, hx-prose, hx-radio — following the established hx-card.mdx pattern.

title: Broken anchor links [Drupal Integration Guide](#) in three component MDX files
impact: Users clicking the Drupal Integration Guide cross-reference links navigate nowhere — the bare # anchor keeps them on the same page. These appear in hx-card.mdx, hx-form.mdx (line 1033), and hx-select.mdx (line 749).
evidence: See the [Drupal Integration Guide](#) in the HELIX documentation for comprehensive coverage — bare hash anchor, non-functional in all three files.
fix_suggestion: Replace with the actual docs site URL using import.meta.env.HELIX_DOCS_URL, or replace with plain text until the target page exists.

title: Deprecated drupal_set_message() used in hx-form.mdx PHP integration example
impact: drupal_set_message() was removed in Drupal 9 and is unavailable in Drupal 10/11 (the stated target platform). Any Drupal developer copying this example will get a fatal PHP error. This is a factual accuracy failure on a platform-critical integration example.
evidence: drupal_set_message(t('Medication order submitted successfully.')); — Drupal 9+ removed this function.
fix_suggestion: Replace with the Drupal 9+ equivalent: \Drupal::messenger()->addStatus(t('Medication order submitted successfully.'));

title: Missing charset and viewport meta tags in preview-head.html
impact: Without charset, browsers may misinterpret multi-byte characters in component labels and documentation. Without viewport, the preview canvas renders with desktop defaults on mobile devices, breaking responsive component testing workflows.
evidence: preview-head.html contains only three Google Fonts link elements. No <meta charset='utf-8'> and no <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1'>.
fix_suggestion: Prepend <meta charset='utf-8' /> and <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1' /> as the first tags in preview-head.html.

title: hx-card.mdx uses wrong attribute name size='sm' instead of hx-size='sm'
impact: Both card documentation examples show <hx-button size='sm'> — the wrong attribute name. The component uses hx-size as confirmed by hx-button.stories.ts. Consumers copying these examples get buttons rendered at default md size, silently ignoring the intended sm size.
evidence: <hx-button size='sm'>View Floor Plan</hx-button> at line 278 and <hx-button size='sm' variant='secondary'>Read More</hx-button> at line 362. Correct: hx-size='sm'.
fix_suggestion: Replace both occurrences with hx-size='sm'. Apply same check to other component MDX files for attribute naming consistency.

title: Production URLs committed in .env.local comments revealing all deployment hostnames
impact: Commented-out values expose https://docs.helix.himer.us, https://storybook.helix.himer.us, and https://admin.helix.himer.us — all production hostnames. Combined with the Vercel token on line 10, an attacker has both targets and credentials. Even post-revocation the hostnames remain discoverable.
evidence: # NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_DOCS_URL=https://docs.helix.himer.us # NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_STORYBOOK_URL=https://storybook.helix.himer.us # NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_ADMIN_URL=https://admin.helix.himer.us
fix_suggestion: Remove the commented-out production block from .env.local. Store production configuration in the Vercel project environment variables UI, not in local files.

title: NEXT_PUBLIC_ environment variable prefix misused in non-Next.js Vite/Storybook context
impact: NEXT_PUBLIC_ is a Next.js-specific prefix. Vite uses VITE_ for automatic browser exposure. Using NEXT_PUBLIC_ in a Vite app creates a naming trap: any developer who accesses import.meta.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_* without the viteFinal define block gets undefined. Also creates inconsistency that could lead developers to assume NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars elsewhere are similarly safe to expose.
evidence: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_HELIX_DOCS_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3150' — NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix in Vite context with no VITE_ equivalent.
fix_suggestion: Rename to STORYBOOK_HELIX_DOCS_URL and STORYBOOK_HELIX_ADMIN_URL (Storybook natively exposes STORYBOOK_* vars). Update .env.local and any CI environment variable configuration.

title: preview-head.html missing noindex meta tag present in manager-head.html
impact: manager-head.html correctly includes <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow' /> but preview-head.html does not. The preview iframe is a separate HTML document. If the preview iframe URL is directly linked or discovered, it will not carry the noindex signal — a defense-in-depth gap.
evidence: manager-head.html line 1: <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow' />. preview-head.html: no equivalent meta tag present.
fix_suggestion: Add <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow' /> as the first line of preview-head.html alongside the charset and viewport additions.

title: Token page CSS transitions have no prefers-reduced-motion override
impact: Colors.mdx, Shadows.mdx, Typography.mdx, and Borders.mdx all use CSS transitions (transform translateY, box-shadow) on hover without @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) overrides. Users with vestibular disorders triggering hover effects on dozens of swatch cards can experience discomfort. Only Introduction.mdx correctly handles this media query.
evidence: .hx-token-colors .hx-colors-swatch { transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease !important; } .hx-colors-swatch:hover { transform: translateY(-2px) !important; } — no prefers-reduced-motion block.
fix_suggestion: Add a global reduced-motion block to preview-head.html: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; } }

title: Infinite DNA helix animation in Introduction.mdx has no pause or stop mechanism
impact: WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) requires that animations lasting more than 5 seconds provide a control to pause or stop them. The helixDnaRotate (20s infinite) and helixOrbDrift animations run continuously with no in-page pause control. prefers-reduced-motion correctly stops them, but users without that OS setting have no mechanism.
evidence: .hx-intro-dna-rotator { animation: helixDnaRotate 20s linear infinite; will-change: transform; } — no pause button, no user control.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden='true' to all animated decorative elements (partially done). Add a 'Pause animations' button or apply animation-play-state: paused on :focus within the hero region.

title: Borders matrix table has no scope attributes and an empty th element
impact: WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships). Screen readers cannot associate table cells with their column/row headers without scope attributes. The combination matrix is inaccessible to NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver users.
evidence: <th className='hx-borders-matrix-th'></th> — empty, no aria-label. Column and row headers lack scope='col' and scope='row' attributes.
fix_suggestion: Add scope='col' to header th elements, scope='row' to row header cells, and aria-label='Border width vs radius' to the empty corner th.

title: Read More button example in card documentation demonstrates inaccessible pattern
impact: The Read More button example provides no context about which card's content will be revealed. Screen reader users navigating a list of cards hear 'Read More, Read More, Read More' with no differentiation. Healthcare teams copying this pattern verbatim ship inaccessible UI.
evidence: <hx-button size='sm'>Read More</hx-button> in card footer slot — no aria-label contextualizing which card.
fix_suggestion: Update the code example to demonstrate the accessible pattern: <hx-button size='sm' aria-label='Read more about {{ node.label }}'>Read More</hx-button> with a documentation note explaining why aria-label is required.

title: Colors.mdx exported functions have untyped parameters — implicit any
impact: resolveHex(token), findSubgroup(label), copyToClipboard(text) — all three parameters are implicitly any. If the token data shape changes, these functions fail at runtime without compile-time warning. MDX TypeScript compilation without tsconfig enforces nothing.
evidence: export function resolveHex(token) { ... } export function findSubgroup(label) { ... } export function copyToClipboard(text) { ... } — no type annotations.
fix_suggestion: Annotate with types from @helix/tokens: resolveHex(token: TokenEntry): string, findSubgroup(label: string): TokenSubgroup | undefined, copyToClipboard(text: string): void.

title: copyToClipboard swallows clipboard errors silently with empty catch handler
impact: If the Clipboard API is unavailable (HTTP context, permissions denied, Safari), the function fails silently. The user clicks a token name and nothing happens. Establishes a bad error-swallowing pattern in documentation examples.
evidence: navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).catch(() => {}); — empty catch handler in both Colors.mdx and Typography.mdx.
fix_suggestion: At minimum add a comment explaining the deliberate silent failure. Ideally show a fallback toast or status message when clipboard write fails.

title: console.log examples in component MDX files establish bad patterns for healthcare consumers
impact: Multiple console.log calls appear in Drupal behavior code examples. Healthcare teams copy documentation examples verbatim. These become console.log calls in production Drupal behaviors running in clinical applications.
evidence: console.log(`Select ${name} changed to: ${value}`); in hx-select.mdx line 558. console.log('Card clicked:', e.detail); in BestPractices.mdx lines 361, 365.
fix_suggestion: Replace console.log in all documentation examples with commented alternatives: // console.log(...) — Use structured logging in production: logger.debug(...)

title: manager.ts uses hardcoded hex colors violating design token mandate
impact: Raw hex values (#2563EB, #8b5cf6, #f8f9fa, etc.) in the manager theme are not synchronized with @helix/tokens. When the design system updates its primary brand color, the Storybook manager chrome will not update. Violates the 'no hardcoded values' non-negotiable.
evidence: colorPrimary: '#2563EB', colorSecondary: '#8b5cf6', appBg: '#f8f9fa', barTextColor: '#6c757d' — all raw hex literals.
fix_suggestion: The Storybook manager context cannot use CSS custom properties. Create a theme-constants.ts file mirroring primitive token values from @helix/tokens as JavaScript constants, used here as the single source of truth.

title: addon-links registered globally but unused across all story and MDX files
impact: @storybook/addon-links registers a global decorator and panel in every story, adding runtime overhead to manager and preview initialization. Zero uses of linkTo, hrefTo, or LinkTo found across all story and MDX files.
evidence: getAbsolutePath('@storybook/addon-links') in addons array — no MDX or story file imports or calls linkTo, hrefTo, or LinkTo.
fix_suggestion: Remove addon-links from addons array in main.ts and from devDependencies in package.json.

title: addon-vitest bundled in production Storybook static site deployment
impact: @storybook/addon-vitest registers test runner UI, Playwright bindings, and vitest adapter code. Including it in the production storybook build (deployed to Vercel) inflates the static site bundle with code that serves no purpose in read-only deployment.
evidence: getAbsolutePath('@storybook/addon-vitest') in addons array. vercel.json deploys the built dist/ — test runner code ships to production.
fix_suggestion: Conditionally load: ...(process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production' ? [getAbsolutePath('@storybook/addon-vitest')] : [])

title: lit listed as devDependency in storybook package.json — risk of dual Lit instantiation
impact: @helix/library already provides Lit as its own dependency. A duplicate Lit instance breaks the element registry (customElements.define throws on duplicate registration), causes instanceof checks to fail across shadow boundaries, and inflates the bundle.
evidence: dependencies: { '@helix/library': '*' } — includes lit. devDependencies: { 'lit': '^3.3.2' } — redundant direct declaration.
fix_suggestion: Remove lit from devDependencies. The import { html } from 'lit' in preview.ts should resolve through the workspace to the single hoisted Lit instance.

title: ~530 lines of duplicated inline CSS across four component MDX files
impact: hx-card.mdx, hx-form.mdx, hx-select.mdx, and BestPractices.mdx each contain ~130-150 lines of nearly identical CSS. The browser re-parses and re-evaluates all CSS rules on every Storybook page navigation. Any visual update requires editing four files.
evidence: hx-card.mdx lines 7-144, hx-form.mdx lines 7-157, hx-select.mdx lines 7-144, BestPractices.mdx lines 7-141 — byte-for-byte identical hero-banner, info-box, warning-box, table, code-block rules.
fix_suggestion: Create stories/styles/docs-shared.css with common rules. Import it once in preview.ts: import '../stories/styles/docs-shared.css'. Remove per-file style blocks.

title: No coverage configuration in storybook vitest.config.ts
impact: No coverage thresholds, collection config, or reporter output. Storybook interaction test results never appear in the Admin Dashboard health scorer. The CLAUDE.md quality gate mandates 80%+ coverage but there is no equivalent gate for the Storybook interaction layer.
evidence: test block has no coverage block. No outputFile, no reporters, no thresholds. Compare to packages/hx-library which has explicit coverage config.
fix_suggestion: Add reporters: ['verbose','json'], outputFile: { json: '.cache/test-results.json' }, coverage: { provider: 'v8', thresholds: { lines: 80, branches: 80, functions: 80, statements: 80 } }

title: 5 critical untested interactions across component story files
impact: 1) hx-form native form reset with ElementInternals — formStateRestoreCallback not verified. 2) hx-select arrow key navigation — no ArrowDown/ArrowUp test. 3) hx-text-input focus on first invalid field during form validation. 4) hx-card click not swallowed by child element clicks. 5) hx-checkbox indeterminate state with aria-checked='mixed'.
evidence: Grep across all story files for indeterminate, ArrowDown, formStateRestore, firstInvalid, scrollIntoView returns no results in any play function body.
fix_suggestion: Delegate to qa-engineer-automation to author the 5 missing interaction test stories. Each should test the specific behavior and verify ARIA state.

title: MDX component pages have no Canvas or Controls live story embeds
impact: All three component overview MDX pages are static documentation with no interactive Canvas blocks. Consumers reading docs see only prose and code blocks — they cannot interact with live story examples inline. The autodocs tab provides some coverage but MDX pages have no direct connection to testable story exports.
evidence: No imports of Canvas or Controls from @storybook/addon-docs/blocks in hx-card.mdx, hx-form.mdx, or hx-select.mdx. No <Canvas> or <Controls> elements anywhere in the files.
fix_suggestion: Import named story exports and embed: import * as CardStories from '../../../packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-card/hx-card.stories.ts'; then <Canvas of={CardStories.Default} /> <Controls of={CardStories.Default} />

title: Missing viewport breakpoint parameters in preview.ts for responsive testing
impact: Without viewport parameters, engineers must manually resize the browser for responsive testing. Healthcare dashboards used on clinical tablets must be verified at common breakpoints. No consistent breakpoint presets defined for the team.
evidence: parameters object has controls, docs, options, a11y, backgrounds — no viewport key.
fix_suggestion: Add viewport: { viewports: { ...INITIAL_VIEWPORTS, tablet: { name: 'Tablet (768px)', styles: { width: '768px', height: '1024px' } }, clinicalDesktop: { name: 'Clinical Desktop (1280px)', styles: { width: '1280px', height: '800px' } } }, defaultViewport: 'responsive' }

title: turbo.json globalDependencies .env.local pattern invalidates entire cache on any env change
impact: globalDependencies: ['**/.env.*local'] causes Turborepo to invalidate the cache for every task in the entire monorepo whenever any .env.local file changes — including the storybook .env.local which contains only localhost dev URLs. A developer changing a local port busts the entire cache.
evidence: 'globalDependencies': ['**/.env.*local'] in turbo.json line 3.
fix_suggestion: Move env var injection to task-level env arrays. Declare only the specific env vars each task actually consumes using the env key in each task definition.

title: Duplicate build and build-storybook scripts with identical bodies
impact: Two scripts execute identical commands: 'build': 'storybook build -o dist' and 'build-storybook': 'storybook build -o dist'. A future change to build flags applied to one will not propagate to the other, creating maintenance drift.
evidence: 'build': 'storybook build -o dist', 'build-storybook': 'storybook build -o dist' — byte-for-byte identical.
fix_suggestion: Make build-storybook delegate to build: 'build-storybook': 'npm run build'. Single source of truth for the actual build command.

title: No URL rewrite rule for SPA routing in vercel.json — deep links return 404
impact: Storybook's client-side routing requires all requests to fall through to index.html. Without a catch-all rewrite, direct URL navigation to story URLs (/story/components-card--default) fails with 404 in production deployment.
evidence: vercel.json has no rewrites or redirects array. Only headers and build config present.
fix_suggestion: Add rewrites: [{ 'source': '/(.*)', 'destination': '/index.html' }] to vercel.json.

title: CDN loading examples use placeholder @1.0.0 version that does not match actual library version
impact: All Drupal integration examples recommend loading from cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@helix/library@1.0.0/dist/index.js. The library's actual version is 0.0.1. Developers copying these examples verbatim attempt to load a version that does not exist on jsDelivr, causing silent 404 and no components loading.
evidence: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@helix/library@1.0.0/dist/index.js — same pattern in hx-form.mdx, hx-select.mdx, and BestPractices.mdx.
fix_suggestion: Replace @1.0.0 with the actual current library version or use a version placeholder comment instructing consumers to substitute their installed version.

title: hx-text-input.stories.ts helpText argType key mismatches CEM attribute help-text
impact: The argType key helpText (camelCase) does not match the component's CEM attribute name help-text (kebab-case). When CEM autodocs processes the component, it generates a help-text row in the Args table separately from the manually declared helpText argType, creating a confusing double-entry in the Controls panel.
evidence: argTypes: { helpText: { control: 'text', ... } } — CEM attribute is help-text. args: { helpText: '' } mapped to render template help-text=${args.helpText}.
fix_suggestion: Rename argType key to 'help-text' with bracket notation to match the CEM attribute name exactly.

title: hx-card.stories.ts wcHref argType key mismatches CEM attribute hx-href
impact: argType key wcHref does not match component attribute hx-href. CEM autodocs will generate an hx-href row separately from the manually declared wcHref control, producing a confusing double-entry and a disconnected control.
evidence: argTypes: { wcHref: { control: 'text', ... } } — rendered as hx-href=${args.wcHref}. CEM attribute is hx-href.
fix_suggestion: Rename wcHref to 'hx-href' (bracket notation) throughout the story file.

title: crossorigin attribute on Google Fonts preconnect should be explicit crossorigin='anonymous'
impact: The bare crossorigin attribute defaults to anonymous per spec but in some proxy or assistive technology configurations the ambiguity can cause the preconnect to fail silently, leading to font loading failure and different rendered sizes for users with specific font rendering settings.
evidence: <link rel='preconnect' href='https://fonts.gstatic.com' crossorigin /> — bare attribute without explicit value.
fix_suggestion: Use the explicit form: crossorigin='anonymous'

title: Shadows.mdx interactive card hover demo has no keyboard-accessible equivalent
impact: WCAG 1.4.13 (Content on Hover or Focus) requires hover-triggered effects to also be available on focus. The shadow transition demonstration is mouse-only. The hint text explicitly says 'Hover over the card to see the shadow transition' confirming no keyboard path exists.
evidence: .hx-shadows-interactive-card:hover { box-shadow: var(--hx-shadow-xl) !important; transform: translateY(-4px) !important; } — no :focus-visible equivalent.
fix_suggestion: Add :focus-visible rule mirroring the :hover state and add tabindex='0' to the card element. Update hint text to say 'Hover or focus the card'.

title: Decorative SVG icons in Introduction.mdx heading elements lack aria-hidden='true'
impact: Screen readers will attempt to read SVG path data or announce 'image' without description when navigating by headings. These SVGs carry no informational content — the heading text provides sufficient context.
evidence:
Sidebar Navigation

— no aria-hidden='true' on SVG.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden='true' to every decorative SVG in Introduction.mdx headings and card headers.

title: New-tab links in Introduction.mdx CTA buttons do not announce new-tab behavior
impact: WCAG 2.4.4 (Link Purpose) and SC 3.2.2 (On Input) require new-tab behavior to be communicated. Screen reader users may become disoriented when a new tab opens unexpectedly — particularly problematic for healthcare professionals relying on assistive technology.
evidence: Documentation — no aria-label or visually-hidden span announcing '(opens in new tab)'.
fix_suggestion: Add aria-label='Documentation (opens in new tab)' to new-tab links. Apply same fix to Admin Dashboard CTA.

title: Borders.mdx uses || instead of ?? for object map lookup — inconsistent with project convention
impact: widthDescriptions[token.key] || 'General purpose border width.' uses || which treats empty string as falsy. Project convention uses ?? for nullish coalescing. Under noUncheckedIndexedAccess the return type is string | undefined — ?? is the correct operator.
evidence: {widthDescriptions[token.key] || 'General purpose border width.'} — should use ?? for consistency with project TypeScript patterns.
fix_suggestion: Change to {widthDescriptions[token.key as BorderWidthKey] ?? 'General purpose border width.'} and type the map keys explicitly as a union type.

title: Package scope @helix/ inconsistent with documented @wc-2026/ convention in CLAUDE.md
impact: CLAUDE.md documents packages as @wc-2026/library but actual package names use @helix/. Creates confusion in Turborepo filter commands, error messages, and workspace references for new team members.
evidence: 'name': '@helix/storybook'. CLAUDE.md states: packages/wc-library — Published as @wc-2026/library to npm.
fix_suggestion: Align CLAUDE.md documentation with actual package names (@helix/*), or align package names with the documented @wc-2026/ scope. Choose one convention and apply consistently.

title: vercel.json missing cache-control headers for content-hashed static assets
impact: Storybook build output includes content-hashed JS/CSS chunks safe to cache aggressively. Without cache headers, browsers re-download these assets on every visit, increasing load time for developers reviewing Storybook on Vercel.
evidence: headers array only has X-Robots-Tag applied globally — no cache-control for hashed assets with immutable directive.
fix_suggestion: Add: { source: '/(.*)\.(js|css|woff2)(.*)', headers: [{ key: 'Cache-Control', value: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }] }

title: No favicon configured for the Storybook manager
impact: Browser tab displays the generic Storybook icon. On a team's bookmark bar or in shared links, the site is visually indistinguishable from any other Storybook instance. Healthcare enterprise contexts commonly have multiple internal tools open simultaneously; a branded favicon is a basic navigability requirement.
evidence: public/ directory contains only robots.txt. No favicon.ico, favicon.svg, or apple-touch-icon.png. No <link rel='icon'> in manager-head.html.
fix_suggestion: Add a branded SVG favicon to public/favicon.svg and reference it: <link rel='icon' type='image/svg+xml' href='./favicon.svg' /> in manager-head.html.

title: manager-head.html missing descriptive title tag — browser tab shows generic Storybook
impact: The browser tab displays 'Storybook' rather than a branded descriptive title. Bookmarks, browser history, and shared links all show the wrong name. brandTitle in manager.ts controls only the sidebar logo text, not the browser tab.
evidence: manager-head.html contains only meta robots tag and Google Fonts links — no <title> tag.
fix_suggestion: Add <title>HELIX Design System — Component Playground</title> to manager-head.html.

title: vitest.setup.ts annotations.beforeAll not guarded against undefined
impact: setProjectAnnotations returns an object where beforeAll may be undefined if no global lifecycle hook is registered. Calling beforeAll(undefined) throws or silently no-ops depending on Vitest version, potentially leaving the test environment uninitialized.
evidence: const annotations = setProjectAnnotations([previewAnnotations]); beforeAll(annotations.beforeAll); — no null guard.
fix_suggestion: Add: if (annotations.beforeAll) { beforeAll(annotations.beforeAll); }

title: storySort in preview.ts explicitly orders only 3 of 13 library components
impact: Components order: ['Button', 'Card', 'Text Input', '*'] — only 3 of 13 components are explicitly ordered. The remaining 10 (Alert, Badge, Checkbox, Form, Container, Prose, Radio Group, Select, Switch, Textarea) sort alphabetically via the wildcard, which may not reflect the desired information architecture.
evidence: order: ['Components', ['Button', 'Card', 'Text Input', '*'], '*'] — 10 components unordered.
fix_suggestion: Add all component names to the sort order to produce a deterministic sidebar order reflecting the designed information architecture.

title: hx-select.mdx uses hardcoded hex values in priority color coding example
impact: Documentation shows style='--hx-select-border-color: #dc2626; --hx-select-label-color: #991b1b;' — hardcoded hex values. Presents the anti-pattern to consumers in official documentation as the recommended approach, directly violating the design token mandate.
evidence: <hx-select label='Order Priority' name='priority' style='--hx-select-border-color: #dc2626; --hx-select-label-color: #991b1b;'>
fix_suggestion: Replace with token references: style='--hx-select-border-color: var(--hx-color-error-600); --hx-select-label-color: var(--hx-color-error-800);'

title: Introduction.mdx missing getting-started or installation section
impact: The introduction page covers navigation and architecture but provides no installation path for developers who want to use HELIX in their own project. A developer landing here for the first time has no way to discover npm install @helix/library or CDN loading from the introduction page.
evidence: Four Quick Links cards cover Design Tokens, Component API, Platform Documentation, Admin Dashboard — no Getting Started or Installation card.
fix_suggestion: Add a Getting Started section with the three installation paths: npm, CDN, and Drupal libraries.yml. This is the first thing a new developer needs.

title: BestPractices.mdx Support and Contribution section contains placeholder contact information
impact: Final section lists GitHub Issues, Slack Channel: Join #helix-drupal, and Documentation without any actual URLs or team identifiers. Generic placeholders that provide no actionable support path — worse than omitting the section entirely.
evidence: ## Support and Contribution - **GitHub Issues**: Report bugs or request features - **Slack Channel**: Join #helix-drupal for community support — no URLs, no repo link.
fix_suggestion: Replace with actual links to the project repository, or remove the section entirely until the information is ready.
